content.claim: Poland's post-reversal PISA cohorts (2022 and later) show the first data point for assessing whether the 2017 re-extension of primary school to 8 years has produced measurable learning outcomes; prior comparisons between 1999 and 2017 effects are methodologically limited because the 2018 cohort was the last to experience the earlier 6+3+3 structure.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Polish system reform lens
limitations: This is a methodological note about data availability, not a causal finding
2022 PISA results exist but their interpretation remains contested in the literature
Long-run labor market, higher education access, and social equity outcomes remain unmeasured for post-reversal cohorts
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
